entries.title: Features and APIs
entries.summary: Android 17 introduces a new `AlarmManager.setExactAndAllowWhileIdle` variant that utilizes an `OnAlarmListener` instead of a `PendingIntent`. This change reduces the need for continuous wakelocks in apps performing periodic tasks like maintaining socket connections, leading to improved battery life and system performance.

The changes include adding a complete Kotlin code example with result handling, data parsing, and helper functions to process the contact picker results and enhanced explanation and examples for single and multiple contact selection, and best practices.

entries.title: Known issues with Android Studio and Android Gradle Plugin
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to include a known issue related to Android Studio Panda 3 and the Develocity IntelliJ plugin v1.2.0. The run action fails due to an incompatibility between the plugin version and the Android Studio version. The issue is tracked in issue #492439205 and a workaround is provided: disabling the Develocity plugin resolves the issue until a new plugin version is available.

entries.title: Release notes for Android Studio preview
entries.summary: This document introduces two new features in Android Studio Panda. The first allows users to extend Agent Mode with specialized skills, as of Canary 2. The second adds Google One integration for Gemini in Agent Mode, providing enhanced features for users of Google One AI Pro or Ultra plans. This integration offers higher rate limits and an expanded context window for the Gemini model.

entries.title: Extend Agent Mode with skills
entries.summary: This new documentation introduces skills for Android Studio's Agent Mode, enabling specialized expertise and custom workflows. It explains how skills, based on the Agent Skills open standard, enhance the agent's capabilities by providing on-demand expertise, repeatable workflows, and efficient use of context windows.

entries.title: SDK Platform Tools release notes
entries.summary: Platform Tools revision 37.0.0 introduces improvements to `adb`. The default mDNS backend has been changed to `libadbmdns`, with `openscreen` as a deprecated failover.  Additionally, `libadbmdns` now provides .local hostnames and the Linux USB backend has been updated to use netlink for device detection, improving performance.

entries.title: Behavior changes: Apps targeting Android 17 or higher
entries.summary: This document outlines behavior changes introduced in Android 17, focusing on performance, security, and privacy. Key changes include a new lock-free implementation of MessageQueue, restrictions on `static final` fields, improvements to accessibility features, enforcement of local network permissions, a new setting for hiding passwords on physical devices, and security enhancements like enabling Certificate Transparency by default and extending Safer Dynamic Code Loading protection to native libraries. It also describes changes related to orientation and resizability restrictions for large screens.

entries.title: Restrictions on orientation and resizability are ignored
entries.summary: The documentation for ff-restrictions-ignored.md was updated to specify that orientation, resizability, and aspect ratio restrictions are ignored for apps targeting Android 17 (API level 37) or higher, on displays with a smallest width greater than 600dp. This clarifies the API level requirement for the described behavior.

entries.title: MessageQueue behavior change guidance
entries.summary: This document describes changes to the `android.os.MessageQueue` implementation in Android 17. The update introduces a lock-free implementation for improved performance, which might affect apps that use reflection on `MessageQueue`. The document clarifies that the new behavior is enabled by default for apps targeting API level 37 and provides instructions on how to test and temporarily disable the new implementation.

entries.title: OTA images for Google Pixel

entries.title: Features and APIs
entries.summary: Android 17 introduces two significant new features: PQC APK Signing and a Dedicated Assistant Volume Stream. PQC APK Signing enhances app security against potential quantum computing attacks by introducing a hybrid APK signature scheme. The Dedicated Assistant Volume Stream provides isolated volume control for Assistant apps, improving user experience and allowing for more flexible audio management.

entries.title: Contact picker

entries.title: Release notes
entries.summary: Android 17 Beta 3 introduces new features and enhancements across various areas, including media and camera, performance and battery, privacy and security, user experience and system UI, and core functionality. Key changes include improvements to the photo picker, RAW14 image format support, granular hearing aid audio routing, a system-provided location button, post-quantum cryptography support, widget support on external displays, and updates to OpenJDK. These updates are targeted at improving app development, user experience, and security.

Key changes include the introduction of BillingClientParameters for SDK versions 26.3.312.0 and later, enabling features like pending purchases, and modifications to the step numbering in the guide. These changes require developers to update their code to align with the new BillingClient instantiation method and to reflect the updated purchase processing steps.

entries.title: Play Games PC SDK
entries.summary: The documentation for the Play Games PC SDK has been updated. The update includes addition of `BillingClientParams` struct, which is used to construct a `BillingClient`.

entries.title: Provide custom back navigation
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to include information on implementing custom back navigation in Jetpack Compose using `BackHandler` composable. It also refactors the section on implementing custom back navigation using Views to include more detail and formatting.  The guidance on using `OnBackPressedCallback` remains the primary method for handling custom back navigation.

entries.title: Get started with large screens
entries.summary: The documentation underwent minor revisions, primarily involving link updates and formatting adjustments. These changes improve the overall presentation and ensure the accuracy of the resources referenced within the document.

entries.title: App to web  |  Large screens  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces the concept of seamlessly transitioning between an Android app and web content to maintain user flow, meeting the Adaptive app quality guidelines for large screens.

entries.title: Camera • audio  |  Large screens  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces guidelines for camera and audio support on large-screen Android devices, focusing on adaptive app quality. It emphasizes the importance of camera and audio switchers, as well as background audio playback, to enhance the user experience on devices with external hardware.

entries.title: Cross device  |  Large screens  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces the concept of cross-device functionality in Android, emphasizing how apps can provide a seamless user experience across different Android devices by meeting adaptive app quality guidelines.

entries.title: Cursors  |  Large screens  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This new documentation introduces the concept of custom cursors in Android apps, particularly for large screens, and explains how to implement them to enhance user interaction and app differentiation.

entries.title: Foldables  |  Large screens  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This new documentation introduces guidelines for adapting Android applications to foldable devices, focusing on features like adaptive layouts, camera support, and the use of Jetpack WindowManager APIs to optimize the user experience on foldable devices.

entries.title: Offline support  |  Large screens  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces offline support for large-screen Android apps, emphasizing the importance of providing functionality even without an active network connection. It guides developers on how to implement offline features to enhance user experience and meet adaptive app quality guidelines.

entries.title: Printing and file management  |  Large screens  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces guidelines for file management and printing functionalities, crucial for adaptive app quality, specifically for large-screen devices.

entries.title: Adaptive differentiated
entries.summary: The documentation has been significantly updated to reflect a major restructuring of the content to focus on desktop experiences and also includes adding or updating the guidelines for user experience, keyboard/mouse/trackpad support, multitasking, drag and drop, and printing/file management. This shift emphasizes the importance of optimizing apps for larger screens and improving the user experience on devices with desktop capabilities.

entries.title: User experience  |  Large screens  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces user experience guidelines for large screens, focusing on making apps adaptive and differentiated to bridge the gap between mobile and desktop experiences. It provides requirements and next steps to enhance the user interface and overall app quality on large-screen devices.

entries.title: Android System Key Verifier

entries.title: Bridging options for notifications
entries.summary: This document provides an overview of how notifications are bridged from a phone app to a Wear OS watch and how to manage duplicate notifications. It covers methods for avoiding duplication, including setting bridge tags, disabling bridging, using dismissal IDs for synchronization, and the use of local-only notifications. It also provides implementation considerations for bridged notifications.

entries.title: Behavior changes: Apps targeting Android 17 or higher
entries.summary: Android 17 introduces changes related to user privacy and security. The major change involves the introduction of the `ACCESS_LOCAL_NETWORK` runtime permission, which is required for apps targeting Android 17 or higher to access the local network, enhancing user privacy by preventing unauthorized access. Another notable change is the refined Background Activity Launch (BAL) restrictions. These changes aim to enhance user security and privacy.

entries.title: Add support for predictive back animations in Views
entries.summary: This document provides guidance on adding custom transitions and animations for predictive back gestures using Views, focusing on using the Progress APIs with AndroidX Transitions. It explains how to use `TransitionManager#controlDelayedTransition` and the `OnBackPressedCallback` methods (`handleOnBackStarted`, `handleOnBackProgressed`, and `handleOnBackPressed`) to create and manage transitions during the predictive back gesture. It also includes code samples and considerations for implementing these transitions.

entries.title: Android developer verification
entries.summary: This document has been updated to reflect changes to the Android Developer Verification program. Key updates include the addition of information about full and limited distribution accounts, and clarified steps developers need to take to get verified, including registering package names. New links to guides, blog posts, and videos have been added, providing more information for developers. The timeline has also been updated to show an earlier start for limited distribution accounts and advanced flow.

entries.title: Android developer verification
entries.summary: This document introduces changes to the Android developer verification process, clarifying requirements, and providing updated timelines. Key changes include enhanced steps for developers, a table detailing different distribution options, and clarifications on key terms and account usage.

entries.title: Register on Android Developer Console
entries.summary: This document describes the process of managing developer identity and app package names outside of Google Play. Key changes include enhanced details on account creation, identity verification requirements (including acceptable documentation), package name registration, transfers, and rules for handling duplicate package names.

entries.title: Frequently asked questions
entries.summary: This update introduces changes to the FAQ documentation related to Android developer verification, including updates to the information on limited distribution, enterprise apps, and advanced flows for sideloading apps. It also adds new FAQs about lost signing keys, multi-key support, and registration without eligible keys. Finally, it adds a new section on advanced flows and sideloading apps for advanced users.

entries.title: Register for full distribution
entries.summary: The new documentation introduces the "full-distribution" account type, designed for organizations and professional developers needing unlimited apps and installs.

entries.title: Register on Google Play Console
entries.summary: The documentation updates clarify the identity verification process and package name registration for Google Play Console developers. Key changes include updating the location to confirm account information in the console, expanding the scope to include registering apps outside of Play, and specifying the availability of auto-registration results for developers starting in March 2026.

entries.title: Register for limited distribution on Android devices
entries.summary: This document introduces a new account type for developers who do not widely distribute their apps. It outlines the features, cost (free), and use cases for this limited-distribution account, which is designed for hobbyists, learners, and educational purposes.

entries.title: Add support for predictive back animations in Views  |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides guidance on adding support for predictive back animations using Views in Android applications. It focuses on utilizing the Progress API and AndroidX Transitions to create custom animations for a more interactive user experience.

entries.title: Modify scenes with scene decorators  |  App architecture  |  Android Developers
entries.summary: This document introduces scene decorators, which allow you to modify scenes calculated by the app's scene strategy. Scene decorators provide a way to encapsulate functionality, such as adding UI components, in a second phase of constructing the content displayed by a NavDisplay.

entries.title: Set up a dispatcher

entries.title: Handle back gestures and predictive back animations

entries.title: Back up large amounts of data with the Android Large Backups API Program
entries.summary: This document introduces the Android Large Backups API, a solution for backing up large amounts of user data, exceeding the 25MB limit of Android Auto Backup. It details the API's benefits, compares it with Auto Backup, and outlines the eligibility criteria and application process for accessing the API, which is targeted at large-scale app developers.

entries.title: DataStore

Additionally, it highlights the introduction of `DataStore.Builder<T>` as a modern alternative to `DataStoreFactory`.

entries.title: Behavior changes: Apps targeting Android 17 or higher
entries.summary: Android 17 introduces a behavior change that makes `static final` fields unmodifiable. This change aims to improve system stability and security by preventing unexpected modification of constant values.

entries.summary: The androidx.collection library documentation has been updated to reflect the release of version 1.6.0. This release includes new features such as no-argument trim functions for MutableFloatList, MutableDoubleList, and MutableIntList, extension functions for creating ScatterMaps and ScatterSets, performance enhancements for ScatterMap and ScatterSet, and an updated minSdk version to 23. These updates provide developers with improved tools for managing collections and optimizing performance in their Android applications.

entries.title: Compose Animation
entries.summary: The compose-animation documentation has been updated to reflect the latest releases. Specifically, version 1.10.5 of the stable release and version 1.11.0-beta01 have been added, including their corresponding commit logs and bug fixes. This update ensures developers have access to the most recent stable and beta versions of the compose-animation library.

entries.title: Compose Foundation
entries.summary: This document details the release of Compose Foundation versions 1.10.5 and 1.11.0-beta01. Version 1.10.5 includes a bug fix for `BasicTextField`, while version 1.11.0-beta01 introduces API changes, bug fixes, and external contributions, including optimizations, new features for trackpad testing, and improvements to text input and tooltip functionality.

entries.title: Compose Material
entries.summary: This document details updates to the Compose Material library.  It announces the release of version 1.10.5 and the beta release of version 1.11.0-beta01, including links to the associated commit logs.  It also updates the code snippets to reflect the latest stable versions.

entries.title: remote compose
entries.summary: This document details the release of Compose Remote version 1.0.0-alpha06. This version includes new features, API changes, and bug fixes related to remote UI creation and rendering, including the addition of new experimental operations for TextStyle, exposure of Remote APIs and bug fixes.

entries.title: Compose Runtime
entries.summary: This document details the updates to the compose-runtime library, including the release of version 1.10.5 and 1.11.0-beta01. These releases include dependency updates and bug fixes, along with API changes in the beta version related to experimental features.

entries.title: Compose UI
entries.summary: This document outlines changes in the Jetpack Compose UI library. Key updates include the release of version 1.10.5, containing bug fixes and minor updates, and the introduction of version 1.11.0-beta01 with new features like custom window token support, preview enhancements, and experimental APIs for adaptive UI development. Several API changes and bug fixes are also documented in the 1.11.0-beta01 release.

entries.title: Compose
entries.summary: The Compose libraries' stable, beta, and alpha versions have been updated. Several libraries, including compose.animation, compose.foundation, compose.material, compose.runtime, and compose.ui, have new stable and beta releases. This provides developers with access to the latest features, bug fixes, and improvements in the Compose UI toolkit.

entries.title: Core Ultra Wideband (UWB)
entries.summary: This document describes the changes made to the core-uwb library, including the introduction of a beta release and new API updates. Version 1.0.0-beta01 is released, along with alpha11. These releases include new APIs for handling ranging failures, UWB state change events, and other feature additions and bug fixes.

entries.title: Core
entries.summary: This document has been updated to reflect the latest stable and release candidate versions of androidx.core libraries. Specifically, the document now indicates Core version 1.18.0 is stable, and Core-location-altitude 1.0.0-rc01 and Core 1.18.0 have been released, along with several other minor updates like changing the compileSdk. The documentation has also been updated to reflect the latest release dates and links to the relevant commits.

entries.title: credentials providerevents
entries.summary: This document details the changes in the credentials-providerevents library. The update includes a new alpha release (1.0.0-alpha06) with API changes, such as the addition of a factory method for RegisterExportRequest and the removal of unused helper methods for ImportCredentialsException.

entries.title: credentials
entries.summary: The documentation for the AndroidX Credentials library was updated to reflect the latest release information. Specifically, the 'Latest Update' date was updated, and the release of 'credentials-e2ee' version 1.0.0-alpha03 was added, including a deprecation notice for `credentials:credentials-e2ee`.

entries.title: DataStore

entries.title: Android Games

It also includes an update to the last updated date and the addition of links to the commit logs for the latest releases. These changes provide the latest stable versions and important context regarding the library's development.

entries.title: Glance Wear
entries.summary: This document outlines the changes in the Glance Wear library.  The update includes the release of version 1.0.0-alpha05, which introduces a new API for Wear Widget background colors and removes the `fetchActiveWidgetsForProvider` method.

entries.title: graphics
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to reflect the release of graphics-path 1.1.0-rc01 and also includes the latest update date. Additionally, there are updates to the dependency declarations using Groovy and Kotlin.

entries.title: ink
entries.summary: This update introduces version 1.1.0-alpha01 of the Ink library, including new features, API changes, and bug fixes. It also includes the release of version 1.0.0, with bug fixes, and details releases from 1.0.0-rc01 to 1.0.0-alpha05. The changes involve improvements to API, and bug fixes.

entries.title: JavascriptEngine
entries.summary: This document introduces version 1.1.0-alpha01 of the JavascriptEngine library, released on March 11, 2026. This alpha release includes bug fixes related to Java 11 compatibility and the use of JSpecify nullness annotations.

entries.title: Lifecycle
entries.summary: This document details the changes in the AndroidX Lifecycle library, specifically focusing on version 2.11.0-alpha02. Key changes include the release of new APIs for scoped `ViewModel` instances in Compose, enabling ViewModels to be tied to specific UI scopes.  This version also introduces new features like support for all KMP targets in ViewModel Navigation3, factory functions for lifecycle owners, and lambda-based lifecycle observer extensions, along with various API additions to support scoped ViewModels and providers.

entries.title: Navigation
entries.summary: The documentation for AndroidX Navigation has been updated to include version 2.10.0-alpha01, released on March 11, 2026. This release introduces new features for predictive back gesture transitions and cross-platform support, along with a minimum SDK update. Additionally, several "Note" and "Caution" sections have been updated to use the "Note" and "Caution" blockquotes.

entries.title: navigation3
entries.summary: The documentation for Navigation 3 has been updated to reflect the release of version 1.1.0-beta01. This update includes the new release date and updated dependency declarations for both Groovy and Kotlin, shifting the dependencies from alpha to beta.

entries.title: Paging
entries.summary: This document introduces version 3.5.0-alpha01 of the Paging library, which includes new features for more flexible data handling and UI integration.  Key additions are a new `asState` flow operator and `append`/`prepend` APIs for `Pager`, providing developers with more control over data loading and state management, as well as new APIs for error handling. These changes enable features such as combining paged data with other data sources, sharing data across multiple collectors without caching, and loading data at the beginning or end of the list.

entries.title: pdf
entries.summary: This update includes the release of androidx.pdf:pdf-viewer-fragment:1.0.0-alpha15. The changes include API changes, such as adding Android SDK Extension Version 19 as a requirement and deprecating `isLinearized`. Bug fixes address issues with the fast scroller and widget interactions, and the libraries now target Java 11.

entries.title: Room 3.0
entries.summary: This document introduces Room 3.0, a new major version of the Room persistence library, focusing on Kotlin Multiplatform (KMP) and introducing changes such as a new package, Coroutine APIs, and support for JavaScript and WasmJS.

entries.title: Savedstate
entries.summary: This update introduces SavedState library version 1.5.0-alpha01, including new features like generic serialization for SavedState objects and extension functions for Kotlin Serializable objects, plus factory functions to reduce boilerplate. It also includes the version information for the latest release.

entries.title: Security
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the release of `androidx.security:security-state:1.1.0-alpha02`. This update includes bug fixes related to System Published SPL and System Modules SPL, ensuring the use of the 'global max' Android Security Bulletin date to support the 'Risk Based Update System' (RBUS).

entries.title: Sqlite
entries.summary: This update introduces version 2.7.0-alpha01, including asynchronous APIs for web targets, a new `sqlite-web` artifact for web worker integration, and various bug fixes and improvements in the 2.6.x series, such as enhanced error codes and performance optimizations.

entries.title: Test Uiautomator
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the release of UI Automator 2.4.0-beta02. This release includes bug fixes, specifically addressing an issue with `UiObject2#scrollToElementOrNull`.

entries.title: Tracing
entries.summary: The tracing.md documentation was updated to reflect the release of tracing-2.0.0-alpha03. This release includes API changes, bug fixes, and details on the included commits.

entries.title: tv
entries.summary: This document details the release of androidx.tv libraries. It includes updates to the release dates and dependency versions for tv-foundation and tv-material.  Significant changes include the introduction of beta versions for tv-foundation and tv-material, and updates to the dependencies to support Kotlin 2.0. Additionally, API changes are documented, including the deprecation of `androidx.compose.ui.graphics.NativePaint` and its replacement with `android.graphics.Paint`.

entries.title: Wear Compose
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to reflect the release of Wear Compose 1.6.0-rc01. This release candidate includes no significant changes compared to the prior beta release, but marks a step towards the final 1.6.0 release.

entries.title: wear protolayout
entries.summary: This update introduces version 1.4.0-rc02 of the wear-protolayout library, released on March 11, 2026. The key change is that applications targeting API 36 and later, which use ProtoLayout Expressions to access heart rate data (e.g., `heartRateBpm`, `heartRateAccuracy`), now require the `android.health.connect.HealthPermissions.READ_HEART_RATE` permission. This change ensures user privacy and data security by requiring explicit permission to access sensitive health information.

entries.title: Wear Tiles
entries.summary: The Wear Tiles documentation was updated to reflect the latest release candidate. Version 1.6.0-rc02 was released, including the release date and a link to the commits. This update ensures developers have access to the most current information regarding the Wear Tiles library.

entries.title: Wear Watch Face Push
entries.summary: The documentation for the Wear Watch Face Push library has been updated to reflect the release candidate version 1.0.0-rc01. This includes updating the release date, dependency declarations, and version information, indicating progress towards a stable release.

entries.title: Webkit
entries.summary: This document details the changes in the `webkit.md` file, which includes updates for the AndroidX Webkit library. The changes introduce new features, API changes, and external contributions in version 1.16.0-alpha03, released on March 11, 2026. This release includes enhancements to web performance metrics, error information, BackForwardCache settings, and the addition of debug information for prefetch requests. It also includes API changes such as increasing the minimum supported SDK, removing experimental features, and migrating OutcomeReceiverCompat.

entries.title: WindowManager
entries.summary: The documentation for the WindowManager library was updated to reflect the latest releases and changes, including new features, API changes, and bug fixes in versions 1.6.0-alpha01, 1.5.1, 1.5.0, and earlier release candidates and betas.  Key changes include the addition of helper methods for constructing `WindowSizeClassSets` in a grid form, the addition of Large and XLarge `WindowSizeClass` breakpoints, expanding the calculation of `WindowMetrics` to the Application Context, providing a getter for direct access to `WindowLayoutInfo`, and introducing APIs for auto-saving and restoring embedding state.  Various bug fixes are also included.

entries.title: Jetpack Compose Glimmer

The update includes the release of version 1.0.0-alpha08, incorporating API changes such as replacing `TitleChipDefaults.contentPadding` with a `ContentPadding` constant, adding `ScrollIndicatorState` to `ListState`, and introducing a new `TextStyle` called `caption` with updates to existing text styles.

entries.title: AndroidX releases

Notable changes include new stable releases for `activity`, `collection`, `compose`, `compose.animation`, `compose.foundation`, `compose.material`, `core`, and `credentials`, along with alpha and beta releases for several other libraries. These updates provide bug fixes, performance improvements, and new features for developers.

This update likely includes bug fixes, performance improvements, or new features within the androidx.core library, which are important for maintaining app stability and utilizing the latest Android features.

Specifically, `tiles-tooling-preview` and `tiles-tooling` dependencies were updated from version 1.6.0-rc01 to 1.6.0-rc02. This change ensures developers are using the most recent tools for previewing and debugging tiles, which might include bug fixes, performance improvements, or new features.

entries.title: Android Gradle Plugin 4.1.0 (August 2020)
entries.summary: The release notes document updates related to the Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) version 4.1.0.  Changes include adjustments to Gradle and SDK Build Tools requirements, and code examples for prefab configurations.

entries.title: Create and monitor geofences
entries.summary: The example XML code snippet for registering a broadcast receiver within the `<application>` tag has been corrected. Specifically, the closing angle bracket was missing in the `android:name` attribute. This fix ensures the receiver is properly registered in the manifest file, which is crucial for the geofencing functionality to work correctly, as it allows the app to receive geofence transition events.

entries.title: Styles in Compose
entries.summary: This document introduces the new Style API for Jetpack Compose, a new way to customize UI elements, offering improved flexibility, performance, and easier design system creation compared to traditional modifiers and styling parameters. It explains the core concepts of Styles, StyleScope, and StyleState, and provides guidance on how to get started with the new API.

entries.title: Do's and don'ts with Styles
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides guidelines, best practices, and patterns for effectively utilizing Styles in Android Compose to maintain consistency and improve code design. It outlines "Do's" and "Don'ts" for visual configurations, design systems, animations, and interaction logic when working with Styles.

entries.title: Examples with Styles
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides examples of using Styles in Android Compose to create custom UI components, specifically focusing on button styling with various effects like hover, depth, and gradient glows, and demonstrating how to apply multiple styles and animations.

entries.title: Fundamentals of Styles
entries.summary: This new documentation introduces the concept of Styles in Jetpack Compose, explaining how to apply them directly to components, through modifiers, and by defining standalone styles for reusability, including inheritance and custom property implementation.

entries.title: Current limitations
entries.summary: This new documentation page outlines the functional limitations of the Styles API in Android Compose, covering aspects like animation support, property scoping, shapes, and interoperability with other UI elements.

entries.title: Performance benefits with Styles
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces Compose Styles and their performance benefits. It explains how Styles optimize rendering by operating in the layout and drawing phases, reducing object overhead, and deferring allocations, leading to improved performance compared to using modifiers.

entries.title: State and animations in Styles
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces the Styles API for managing UI changes based on interaction states like hovered, focused, and pressed, streamlining the process and reducing boilerplate code, with built-in animation support.

entries.title: Styles versus modifiers
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces the concept of Styles in Jetpack Compose, comparing and contrasting them with Modifiers, outlining their respective strengths and limitations, and providing guidance on when to choose one over the other for UI development. It explains that Styles are specialized Modifiers primarily designed for visual appearance, theming, and animation optimization, whereas Modifiers are more suitable for defining behaviors, complex layouts, and adding functionalities.

entries.title: Image keyboard support
entries.summary: The code now correctly checks the Android API level using `>=` instead of `=` for `Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.N_MR1` in both Kotlin and Java examples. This ensures the correct behavior when granting read URI permission for input content.

entries.title: Track a device's pose using ARCore for Jetpack XR
entries.summary: The documentation for device pose has been updated with code samples and clarified explanations. Specifically, code examples now use `deviceTracking` instead of `deviceTrackingMode` and provide two methods for obtaining the device pose: once or continuously via a collector. Additionally, a newline and example code was added after the `processDevicePose` function call. The note about the `HEAD_TRACKING` permission has been updated to use a URL rather than a Markdown link.

entries.title: Work with geospatial poses using ARCore for Jetpack XR
entries.summary: The documentation for the Geospatial API was updated to improve code clarity and provide better examples. The changes include code formatting improvements, updated code snippets, and additional context to aid in understanding the API's usage.

entries.title: Jetpack Navigation 3 Skill
entries.summary: This document has been updated to reflect changes in the navigation guide, including updated links and descriptions. Notably, several recipes have been updated to provide better examples and instructions for common use cases. Furthermore, recipes using Scenes have been updated to reflect the new API and a new 'Material Adaptive' section has been added. These changes improve the clarity and usefulness of the navigation documentation.

entries.title: Read aggregated data
entries.summary: The documentation for aggregating data in Health Connect was updated to use a TIP and NOTE callout style for better formatting and readability. The formatting change does not affect the content's technical accuracy.

entries.title: Health Connect data types
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to include a 'Data type fields' table that clarifies the structure and requirements of each data type.  This table describes fields such as record type, unit of measurement, and mandatory fields to maintain data integrity.  The table includes a list of the mandatory fields for each type to improve clarity and assist developers.

entries.title: Develop Sleep Experiences with Health Connect
entries.summary: This new documentation details how to build sleep tracking experiences within Android apps using Health Connect, covering data types, permissions, background execution, and recommended workflows for comprehensive sleep data management.

entries.title: Develop Vitals Experiences with Health Connect
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides a guide on how to build apps that manage user vitals data using Health Connect, covering permissions, read/write workflows, and best practices for data management.

entries.title: Develop Workout Experiences with Health Connect
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides a guide for developers on building workout tracking experiences using Health Connect, covering data types, background execution, permissions, workflows, and best practices for creating comprehensive workout features.

entries.title: Add exercise routes
entries.summary: The documentation for exercise routes has been updated to clarify permission handling. The note regarding permission behavior was updated to use a note block, and the guidance on checking for granted permissions has been made more explicit to ensure that apps handle scenarios where permission is lost before using any permission. These changes improve clarity and reinforce best practices for handling user permissions within the Health Connect framework.

entries.title: Track mindfulness
entries.summary: The documentation for mindfulness sessions in Health Connect has been updated. The changes include correcting the prompt for checking permissions and providing a new code snippet demonstrating how to read mindfulness sessions within a specified time range, including how to process the returned records. Additionally, the code for writing mindfulness sessions is included.

entries.title: Measure skin temperature
entries.summary: The documentation for skin temperature in Health Connect was updated to use a note block and clarify that skin temperature data is not for core body temperature measurement. The documentation also stresses the importance of regularly checking for permissions, which is critical for ensuring that an app can use sensitive health data.

entries.title: Track sleep sessions
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to clarify the difference between subtype and associated data in sleep sessions. Associated data, unlike subtype data, is recorded independently and has its own UUID. The example code for adding sleep stages has been enhanced with complete stage definitions, including specific start and end times, and timezone offsets. Additionally, the permission check reminder was updated and the formatting of the notes section was changed.

entries.title: Track steps
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to reflect best practices for permission handling. The guide now recommends checking permissions before use and handling scenarios where permissions are lost, which is critical for maintaining app functionality and data access.

entries.title: Training plans

entries.title: Get started with Health Connect
entries.summary: This document introduces several changes, including the update of `Note` to `> [!NOTE]` and `Important` to `> [!IMPORTANT]` blocks, and the addition of a clarification on permission checks, ensuring they are performed before using the permissions. These changes aim to improve the clarity and formatting of the documentation, as well as to improve the best practice guidance for Health Connect app developers.

entries.title: Read medical data
entries.summary: The documentation for reading medical data in Health Connect has been updated. The changes include simplifying the step to read data, clarifying how to obtain medical records, improving the examples for getting medical records by ID, and including a new section explaining the MedicalDataSource. These updates provide clearer guidance on data retrieval and the use of different data sources.

entries.title: Write medical data
entries.summary: The documentation for writing medical data in Health Connect has been updated. Key changes include clarifying permission handling by emphasizing the need to check for permissions before use, and refining the note and caution messages when deleting MedicalDataSource and MedicalResource records, along with minor formatting and code example updates.

entries.title: Publish your health app on Google Play

It clarifies concepts like `suspend`, `resume`, and `withContext` to write clean, simplified asynchronous code.

entries.title: Best practices for coroutines in Android

entries.title: Best practices for coroutines in Android

entries.title: Best practices for coroutines in Android
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to clarify that suspend functions should be main-safe, and the responsibility of moving execution off the main thread lies within the class doing the work.

entries.title: Best practices for coroutines in Android

entries.title: Best practices for coroutines in Android

entries.title: StateFlow and SharedFlow
entries.summary: This document introduces `StateFlow` and `SharedFlow`, Kotlin Flow APIs for managing state updates and emitting values to multiple consumers. It covers their usage, characteristics, and differences, including how to use `shareIn` to make cold flows hot and how to customize `SharedFlow` behavior.  The document also provides important notes and warnings for developers, clarifying the correct way to handle flow collection within the UI, and provides links to further resources.  Several minor formatting changes and markdown improvements were made throughout the document, improving readability.

entries.title: Common problems and solutions
entries.summary: This document provides a guide to common problems encountered when using the NDK and their solutions. It covers issues related to `_FILE_OFFSET_BITS=64`, `minSdkVersion`, symbol resolution, atomic operations, RTTI/exceptions across library boundaries, and using mismatched prebuilt libraries.

entries.title: Common problems and solutions
entries.summary: The document explains that using `_FILE_OFFSET_BITS=64` with older API levels may result in fewer functions being available. This is because `mmap64` was not available until Android 21. If the `minSdkVersion` is lower than 21, the C library does not contain an `mmap` that is compatible with `_FILE_OFFSET_BITS=64`.

entries.title: Common problems and solutions
entries.summary: The document provides a solution to the problem where exceptions are not being caught or `dynamic_cast` is failing across shared library boundaries. The recommended solution is to add a key function to your types. When a type does not have a key function, its typeinfo is emitted as a weak symbol and matching type infos are merged when libraries are loaded. When loading libraries dynamically, it may not be possible for the loader to merge type infos for the loaded libraries.
